prs.title: ENH: Plot overhaul for group_difference (#3799)
prs.body:
## Overview

Closes #3799

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:

Updates `shap.plots.group_difference` to follow the explicit matplotlib Axes interface as part of #3411. The plot now accepts a typed optional `ax` argument, creates an Axes only when one is not provided, and returns the Axes object for downstream customization.

This also fixes the documented 1D model-output vector path by normalizing the input shape before computing confidence bounds. Previously, `xerr` was computed before reshaping 1D inputs, which could lead to invalid indexing during plotting.

Tests cover:
- returning a matplotlib Axes
- preserving and returning a user-provided Axes
- rendering through pytest-mpl on a custom Axes
- the 1D model-output vector path, including `show=True`, `max_display`, `sort=False`, and custom axis bounds/label



## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

prs.title: ENH: Plot overhaul for embedding (#3796)
prs.body:
## Overview

Closes #3796

Description of the changes proposed in this pull request:

Updates `shap.plots.embedding` to use the explicit matplotlib Axes interface as part of #3411. The plot now accepts an optional `ax` argument, draws through the provided/current Axes, uses the owning Figure for colorbar and sizing operations, and returns the Axes object for downstream customization.

This also adds type hints, `from __future__ import annotations`, a clear `ValueError` for unsupported embedding methods, and tests covering return type, custom Axes identity, PCA behavior, unsupported methods, and pytest-mpl rendering on a custom Axes.



## Checklist

- [x] All [pre-commit checks](https://pre-commit.com/#install) pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

prs.title: ENH: Plot overhaul for heatmap — explicit matplotlib Axes interface (#3411)
prs.body:
## Overview
Closes #3800, part of #3411.

Completes the remaining checklist items for the heatmap plot overhaul:

- Add `from __future__ import annotations` so the `X | Y` union type syntax works on Python < 3.10
- Add type hints to `max_display`, `show`, `plot_width`, `ax`, and return type (`-> plt.Axes`)
- Replace `plt.colorbar()` with `ax.get_figure().colorbar()` — the last remaining implicit pyplot call in this function

The `ax` parameter and `return ax` were already present. This PR completes what was missing.

AI usage disclosure: I used Claude for guidance and did almost all of the work by myself. I reviewed and understood every line; the from __future__ import enables union type syntax on Python < 3.10, the type hints follow the same pattern as _beeswarm.py, and replacing plt.colorbar() with ax.get_figure().colorbar() removes the last implicit pyplot call in this function

## Checklist
- [x] All pre-commit checks pass.
- [x] Unit tests added (if fixing a bug or adding a new feature)

prs.title: refactor: migrate 404 error page from deprecated makeStyles to MUI v5 styled API
prs.body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of your changes in the Title above -->

## Description

Migrates src/components/ErrorPages/404.jsx from the deprecated @mui/styles makeStyles API to the MUI v5 styled() API. Removes the inline useStyles hook and replaces className references with three styled components: Wrapper, ErrorImage, and OopsList. Also updates nth-child selectors to nth-of-type and improves image alt text for accessibility.

## Related Issue

Part of #314 (refactor: Migrate deprecated MUI v4 components to MUI v5)

## Motivation and Context

The codebase still uses makeStyles from @mui/styles in several components. This is one of the causes of the peer dependency conflicts during npm install and blocks the full TypeScript migration. Migrating to the MUI v5 styled() API removes the deprecated dependency usage and brings the component in line with the rest of the ongoing migration effort.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- Navigated to a non-existent route (localhost:5173/test) to trigger the 404 page
- Page renders correctly with no visual regression
- No new console errors introduced by this change
- npm run dev runs successfully

prs.title: refactor: migrate Footer component from deprecated makeStyles to MUI v5 styled API
prs.body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of your changes in the Title above -->

Migrates src/components/Footer/index.jsx from the deprecated @mui/styles makeStyles API to the MUI v5 styled() API as part of the ongoing MUI v4 → v5 migration.

## Related Issue

Part of #314 (refactor: Migrate deprecated MUI v4 components to MUI v5)

The Footer component was using makeStyles from @mui/styles which is deprecated in MUI v5 and contributes to the peer dependency conflicts seen during npm install. prs.title: Feat/UI improvement mini navbar
prs.body:
## Description
Fixes three pre-existing bugs in the SideBar component group, 
all related to the ongoing MUI v4 → v5 migration.

## Changes

**sidelist.jsx:**
- Fixed hardcoded `key="menu-items"` on every mapped div — every 
  item was getting the same key, causing duplicate key warnings
- Migrated from deprecated `makeStyles` (@mui/styles) to MUI v5 
  `styled()` API
- Fixed children rendering inside MenuList using React.Children.map 
  to avoid Fragment warning

**SideBar/index.jsx:**
- Removed Fragment wrapper (`<>...</>`) from return statement — 
  this was causing "MUI: Menu doesn't accept Fragment as child" warning
- Migrated from deprecated `makeStyles` to MUI v5 `styled()` API
- Simplified conditional rendering to return single elements directly

## Related Issue
Closes #314

## How Has This Been Tested?
- Ran npm run dev — no duplicate key warnings in console
- MUI Fragment warning resolved
- No functional regressions introduced
- App builds and runs correctly on localhost:5173

## Types of changes
- [x] Bug fix (non-breaking change which fixes an issue)

prs.title: feat: add production multi-stage Dockerfile and update docker-compose
prs.body:
<!--- Provide a general summary of your changes in the Title above -->

## Description

Upgrades the Docker setup as part of Pre-GSoC 2026 Task 2 (Dockerization).

## Related Issue

Part of #283 (Pre-GSoC 2026 tasks - Task 2: Dockerization)

## Motivation and Context

The existing Dockerfile used deprecated Node 14 in a single stage, 
serving the dev server in production. This PR introduces a proper 
multi-stage build:

- Stage 1 (builder): Node 18 Alpine installs dependencies and 
  builds the production bundle via npm run build
- Stage 2 (production): Nginx Alpine serves the static dist/ 
  output with proper routing and asset caching

Additional changes:
- Dockerfile.dev for local development with hot reload
- nginx.conf with React Router support and gzip compression
- Updated docker-compose.yml with separate prod and dev services
- .dockerignore to keep image size minimal

## How Has This Been Tested?

- npm run build completes successfully (16039 modules transformed)
- Production build outputs to dist/ correctly
- Multi-stage Dockerfile verified to build without errors

prs.title: Created prototype website
feat: migrate CLI runtime to pnpm (POC)
prs.body:
**Description**

This PR implements a migration from `npm` to `pnpm` to improve CI/CD performance and dependency stability.

**Key Improvements:**
- **Performance:** Reduced fresh installation time from **~4m 13s** (npm) to **~1m 19s** (pnpm) — a **3.2x speedup**.
- **Stability:** Identified and fixed several "phantom dependencies" that `npm` was hoisting implicitly but `pnpm` flagged as missing. Explicitly added:
  - `tslib`
  - `ajv`
  - `ajv-formats`
  - `jsonfile (Fixed build error in src/apps/cli/commands/new/template.ts)`
  - `@stoplight/spectral-core`
  - `@stoplight/spectral-formatters`
  - `@apidevtools/json-schema-ref-parser`

**Verification:**
- `pnpm install` completes successfully.
- `pnpm run cli:test` passes all tests locally.

**Related issue(s)**
Fixes #2023
Relates to #1991

prs.title: Fix JSON logs missing time units by adding human-readable response time
prs.body:
This PR adds human-readable time units to JSON-formatted logs to match the terminal pretty-printed logs.

Changes:-
- Added a new response_time_human field to JSON logs, using duration.String() to include units (µs, ms, s).
- Preserved the existing numeric response_time field (in microseconds) for backward compatibility.
- Ensures complete parity between JSON logs and terminal logs while maintaining non-breaking changes.

Fixes:- #2480

prs.title: fix: enforce compile-time error for string date inputs
prs.body:
This PR resolves a TypeScript typing issue in format() where string date inputs were incorrectly accepted at compile time, despite always failing at runtime.

What was fixed
- Removed the incorrect TypeScript overload that allowed a string as a date argument.
- Added a correct JSDoc overload so TypeScript marks string inputs as never.
- Resolves the TS2394 overload mismatch error.

Why this matters
- TypeScript now blocks invalid usage like format("2020-01-01", ...).
- Behavior is consistent with date-fns v3 design and runtime expectations.
- No impact on runtime behavior; improves type safety only.

fixes: #4033

prs.title: Fix: Remove Dark Mode Inversion Filter to Preserve Accurate Canvas & Image Colors
prs.body:
This PR resolves an issue where imported images and canvas elements appeared washed out, inverted, or color-shifted when switching to Dark Mode in Excalidraw.

The root cause was the use of a global CSS inversion filter (invert() + hue-rotate()) applied indirectly through the --theme-filter variable during dark mode.
This filter also affected the <canvas> and imported images, resulting in inaccurate colors.

This PR removes the inversion behavior entirely, ensuring that both light and dark themes render colors faithfully.

What Was Changed
1. Disabled the global theme inversion filter

Replaced:
`
--theme-filter: invert(93%) hue-rotate(180deg);
`

with:
`
--theme-filter: none;
`

for both light and dark themes.


2. Ensured neutral filter behavior across .excalidraw

3. Ensured Dark Mode uses correct non-inverted colors

Updated the .theme--dark block to explicitly set:
`
--theme-filter: none;
`

Visual Impact

Before:-
- Imported images appear lighter, inverted, or off-color
- Canvas export does not match user-visible colors
- Color bars (e.g., RGB stripes) shift significantly in dark mode

After:-
- Imported images retain original colors
- Canvas and exports match exactly
- UI still respects dark mode styling via CSS variables

No filter is applied to the canvas or any drawing elements

Fixes:- #10373

prs.title: fix: Correct version mismatch causing noUpdateNotifier to fail
prs.body:
This pull request fixes a bug where setting "noUpdateNotifier": true in turbo.json did not suppress update notifications.
Turbo continued printing out-of-date version warnings regardless of the flag’s value.
What was happening
Even when users explicitly disabled update notifications using:
`{
  "noUpdateNotifier": true
}`
Turbo still displayed upgrade messages when the installed version was behind the latest available version.
This violated the expected behavior described in Issue #10940.
What this PR does:
- Ensures that when noUpdateNotifier is set to true, Turbo skips the update-notifier check entirely.
- Prevents unnecessary noise in CI pipelines and automated scripts.
- Restores expected behavior and aligns with Turbo’s configuration contract.

How I verified the fix:
Added "noUpdateNotifier": true to turbo.json.
Ran Turbo using an intentionally outdated version.
Confirmed that no update notification was printed.
Verified that notifications still appear when the flag is not set.

Linked Issue
Fixes: #10940

<sub>CLOSES TURBO-4878</sub>

prs.title: fix: set root directory to project root for proper deployment
prs.body:
### Summary
Fixed deployment issue on Vercel by aligning the build output to the project root. Previously, `outputDirectory` pointed to `excalidraw-app/build`, which caused failed deployments unless the Vercel Root Directory was manually set.

### Changes Made
- Updated `vercel.json` to set `outputDirectory` to `"build"`.
- Adjusted `installCommand` to `yarn install && yarn build:app` for proper build step execution.

### Related Issues
- Fixes #9155
- Related: #7139, #8734

### Testing
- Deployed successfully to Vercel without modifying the Root Directory.
- Confirmed that the app builds and serves correctly.

prs.title: fix: Make with-microfrontends cross-platform using cross-env
prs.body:
This PR fixes the with-microfrontends example so it runs correctly on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Problem:-
When running:
```
pnpm run dev
```

On Windows, the example failed with:
```
error: option '-p, --port <port>' argument '$TURBO_MFE_PORT' is invalid
```

Fix:-
Added cross-env for environment variable handling
Updated package.json scripts for docs, web, and vite apps:
```
"dev": "cross-env TURBO_MFE_PORT=300x next dev --port $TURBO_MFE_PORT"
```

Added packageManager field to avoid missing field errors

Verified on macOS and Windows — works perfectly.

Result
-> pnpm run dev now runs successfully on all platforms
-> Each app starts on separate ports (3001, 3002, 3003)
-> No more $TURBO_MFE_PORT invalid errors

issues.title: bug: Duplicate keys in SideList, MUI Fragment warning and deprecated makeStyles in SideBar components
issues.body:
**Describe the bug**
Three related bugs exist in the SideBar component group that cause 
console warnings and contribute to the MUI v4/v5 version conflict.

1. src/components/SideBar/sidelist.jsx uses key="menu-items" 
hardcoded on every div in the map function, giving every list item 
the same key and causing React's duplicate key warning.

2. src/components/SideBar/index.jsx wraps its entire return in a 
Fragment (<>...</>). MUI's MenuList does not accept a Fragment as 
a child, producing the warning: "MUI: The Menu component doesn't 
accept a Fragment as a child. Consider providing an array instead."

3. Both files use makeStyles from @mui/styles which is deprecated 
in MUI v5 and is one of the causes of the peer dependency conflicts 
seen during npm install.

**To Reproduce**
Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Clone the repo and run npm install --legacy-peer-deps
2. Run npm run dev
3. Open browser DevTools console
4. See warnings: "Encountered two children with the same key" 
   and "MUI: The Menu component doesn't accept a Fragment as a child"

**Expected behavior**
Each menu item should have a unique key. The SideBar should render 
without Fragment warnings. Both components should use MUI v5 styled() 
API instead of deprecated makeStyles.

**Desktop (please complete the following information):**

- OS: macOS
- Browser: Chrome
- Version: Latest

**Additional context**
A fix for all three issues is available in PR #405.

issues.title: bug: Duplicate keys in SideList map, MUI Fragment warning in SideBar, and deprecated makeStyles usage
issues.body:
## Describe the bug

Three related bugs exist in the SideBar component group:

**1. Duplicate keys in SideList**
`src/components/SideBar/sidelist.jsx` uses `key="menu-items"` 
hardcoded on every div in the map function. This gives every 
item the same key, causing React's duplicate key warning and 
potential rendering issues.

**2. MUI Fragment warning in SideBar**
`src/components/SideBar/index.jsx` wraps its return in a 
Fragment (`<>...</>`). MUI's MenuList does not accept a Fragment 
as a child, causing the console warning:
"MUI: The Menu component doesn't accept a Fragment as a child."

**3. Deprecated makeStyles usage**
Both `sidelist.jsx` and `index.jsx` use `makeStyles` from 
`@mui/styles` which is deprecated in MUI v5 and causes peer 
dependency conflicts.

## Fix
All three issues are addressed in PR #405.

## Labels
bug, dependency, UI/UX

issues.title: [FEATURE] Migrate CLI runtime to pnpm for improved install performance
issues.body:
### Why do we need this improvement?

The current npm installation process for the CLI is significantly slow, taking approximately 4m 13s on a fresh install (local benchmark). This creates friction for new contributors and slows down CI/CD pipelines where fresh installs occur frequently.

Furthermore, npm's hoisting mechanism is currently masking several missing peer dependencies (e.g., ajv, tslib, spectral). This results in a "fragile" dependency graph where the project relies on packages that aren't explicitly declared in package.json.

### How will this change help?

- Performance: Migrating to pnpm reduces fresh installation time to ~1m 19s. This is a ~3.2x speed improvement.

- Stability: pnpm uses a strict dependency resolution strategy. This forces us to explicitly declare all used dependencies, preventing "phantom dependency" bugs and ensuring the build is deterministic across all environments.

### Screenshots

_No response_

### How could it be implemented/designed?

- Remove package-lock.json.

- Generate pnpm-lock.yaml.
- Update package.json to include the missing dependencies identified during the migration (specifically: tslib, ajv, ajv-formats, @stoplight/spectral-core, @stoplight/spectral-formatters, @apidevtools/json-schema-ref-parser).
- Update CI workflows to use pnpm install instead of npm install.

### 🚧 Breaking changes

Yes

### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues?

- [x] I checked and didn't find a similar issue

### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines?

- [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md)

### Are you willing to work on this issue?

Yes I am willing to submit a PR!

issues.title: [BUG] UnparsedCommand warnings in test logs for versions and convert commands
issues.body:
### Describe the bug.

When running npm run cli:test, the logs show [UnparsedCommand] Warning for the config:versions and convert commands.

### Expected behavior

Commands should parse arguments correctly to avoid console warnings during tests.

### Screenshots

N/A - See logs provided above.

### How to Reproduce

Run `npm run cli:test` and observe the output.

### 🖥️ Device Information [optional]

_No response_

- [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue

### Are you willing to work on this issue ?

Yes I am willing to submit a PR!
